name: Matt Gaetz
subject: Remember and Honor 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF MATT GAETZ
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Patriot,
Today is a special day for America. We have to honor of celebrating and thanking the brave men and women who have valiantly fought for our country, our heroes.
---
|
---
| It is because of these patriots, that we get to enjoy the freedoms we have today. Don't forget to honor those who have fought for you!
If you are a veteran or are currently serving our country, thank you, you are a hero.

Thank you for your service,
---
|
---
| Matt Gaetz
---
| |
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0015xAvKETIahZ7-erMg1TyxLr4I6lpfl9-q3cmcrn-Lek3yxfLiPVM3uvnxYuY3tzu_mP45aI73cUEiJPiGYRZNZj8Nsbo_weedA73yj-Hk4bzVyLGvRxjUOnC0Iv3t05VoN7gYHccNZ8RJvfkvgf5tYqFE7AiIfuiDDFSXtLlbHY2Ss2PhW7QoA==&c=KgYCHuWPpxrfEaouDPfy1CcjTsxnVilebB-hNjEDBKub8lC6Vd3TBg==&ch=Yh6XmxxCCJaBUx7G9v_b0shZAUkfIZKa5fQY87YhEmwjFz9W6ydlBg==) [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0015xAvKETIahZ7-erMg1TyxLr4I6lpfl9-q3cmcrn-Lek3yxfLiPVM3uvnxYuY3tzu86DGd63euEDf-6Z90E0OFBZyrr4lgk225GXbRPWVqFqcwRpbeFAONlInzxfDamxFhW-LPUbDXZ43BFCeviqX4g==&c=KgYCHuWPpxrfEaouDPfy1CcjTsxnVilebB-hNjEDBKub8lC6Vd3TBg==&ch=Yh6XmxxCCJaBUx7G9v_b0shZAUkfIZKa5fQY87YhEmwjFz9W6ydlBg==)

name: Jason Yates
subject: This speech set the precedent
body:
| | A message from President James A. Garfield for this Memorial Day
---
| Peter, In 1868 Congressman James A. Garfield, who would later become the 20th president of the United States, gave a speech on “Decorations Day” at Arlington National Cemetery, the day we now refer to as Memorial Day. Congressman Garfield served as a Major General in the Union Army before being elected to Congress. He had a front-row seat to the tremendous sacrifices of those who gave their lives for freedom and the union. On that first official “Decorations Day” people lined the rows of tombstones with flowers at Arlington National Cemetery as an act of remembrance, respect, and gratitude; Congressman Garfield addressed a crowd of about 5,000 people gathered there. _“I am oppressed with a sense of the impropriety of uttering words on this occasion. If silence is ever golden, it must be here beside the graves of fifteen thousand men, whose lives were more significant than speech, and whose death was a poem, the music of which can never be sung. With words we make promises, plight faith, praise virtue. Promises may not be kept; plighted faith may be broken; and vaunted virtue be only the cunning mask of vice. We do not know one promise these men made, one pledge they gave, one word they spoke; but we do know they summed up and perfected, by one supreme act, the highest virtues of men and citizens. For love of country they accepted death, and thus resolved all doubts, and made immortal their patriotism and their virtue._ _“For the noblest man that lives, there still remains a conflict. He must still withstand the assaults of time and fortune, must still be assailed with temptations, before which lofty natures have fallen; but with these the conflict ended, the victory was won, when death stamped on them the great seal of heroic character, and closed a record which years can never blot.”_ Garfield concluded by saying, _“What other spot so fitting for their last resting place as this under the shadow of the Capitol saved by their valor? Here, where the grim edge of battle joined; here, where all the hope and fear and agony of their country centered; here let them rest, asleep on the Nation’s heart, entombed in the Nation’s love!”_ It is our responsibility to ensure the sacrifices of millions who gave their lives for our country are not forgotten. We cannot reflect on this holiday without also remembering the ultimate example of the perfect sacrifice made by Jesus Christ on the cross. John 15:13 says, “Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.” Those who have died in service to our nation remind us of that truth. We encourage you to take time today to thank God for the tremendous freedom we enjoy in America. May the examples of those who have given their lives in sacrifice inspire in us the selfless love of our Lord Jesus Christ. And pray for the families of our fallen troops, that their homes and their lives would be filled with God’s strength and peace. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190527com01&n=1)
* * *
[Support My Faith Votes with your tax deductible donation.](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idsHszZ_cNjdrkrk6Ct22R5LqpO-pYuvVIOOj4RsxM_Q&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190527com01&n=2) _My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement that motivates, equips and activates Christians in America to vote in every election, transforming our communities and influencing our nation with biblical truth. By partnering with national faith leaders, My Faith Votes provides resources to help Christians Pray, Think, and Act to create an America where God is honored in the public square._
---
* * *

10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sQ5cX_bEu8cPkWYJ2Mr5-wZKXu3dY5XWtaPM4h3adih&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190527com01&n=4) [ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=pQzeq3ow3I_GdDaQAo3jThGOsBJSnRMSTnYdqF5RrFw9RIPs8jarlLWzFCwppO5s&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190527com01&n=5) [ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6hyvAYXBs1PELL28GD73y-k8vVr8cJ4CYXZKhF1mHHVF_9enIccjId0FBdBJJ7Kr&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190527com01&n=6)

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: honor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Florida Action,
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3589/9e00690/6a2ef8bd/24077430/259487821/VEsH/ "https://forwardfla.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=052719-es-memorial-day-nfr&source=052719-es-memorial-day-nfr")
On Memorial Day, we honor the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice serving our nation.
On Memorial Day, we honor the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice serving our nation. The heroes who gave their lives for our country to protect our freedoms and keep us safe is a debt we can never repay.

Sacrifices were not only made by the courageous men and women we lost; the families of these heroes also carry this sacrifice with them.

My heart goes out to all the families of these brave men and women who gave everything, and it's our solemn duty to support them as they live with this loss.

We must always remember the valiant Americans who fight to protect our nation, those we have lost, and those who are risking everything to protect us right now.

Today and every day, I hope you will join me in keeping these families in your prayers and honor these fallen heroes who deserve our full and unyielding gratitude.

Andrew Gillum

---

Paid for by Forward Florida Action,
1427 Piedmont Drive E., Suite 2, Tallahassee FL, 32308.
---

Contributions to Forward Florida Action are not tax-deductible.
Forward Florida Action's mission is to educate the public on progressive policy solutions and to increase voter registration and voter participation in Florida.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Forward Florida Action. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, and we'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3589/9e00690/6a2ef8bd/24077433/259487821/VEsE/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+unsubscribe&utm_campaign=052719-es-memorial-day-nfr&source=052719-es-memorial-day-nfr").

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
We're grateful. And here's part of how we show it: Doing everything we can to make sure the decisions made in Washington are in the best interest of our service members, their families, and our nation | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e56a/4037968354/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
When I was growing up, Memorial Day was a really big holiday in my family.

All three of my big brothers served in the military. My oldest brother was career military and was deployed to Vietnam over a five year period.

I grew up with my mother always watching for the mail. When a letter came from one of the boys, the day was extra-sunny. She would read it and re-read it, and call my Aunt Max or my grandmother to read it again over the phone.

My brothers all made it home safely, and for that, I'm eternally grateful.

It gave Memorial Day a special meaning: Understanding what others have lost, and honoring even more the sacrifice of those who died in service to our country and those who love them. We owe them a debt that can never be repaid.

We're grateful. And here's part of how we show it: Doing everything we can to make sure the decisions made in Washington are in the best interest of our service members, their families, and our national security. Taking care of our veterans when they come home. And fighting to bring these forever wars to a close.

**No matter how you honor Memorial Day, I hope you have a meaningful day.**

Elizabeth

| | | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e56d/4037968354/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/ElizabethWarren/") | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e56c/4037968354/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/ewarren") | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e56f/4037968354/VEsC/ "https://www.instagram.com/elizabethwarren/") | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e56e/4037968354/VEsD/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrj-0FxK19tgUR2EbHkBBGg/videos") | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e551/4037968354/VEsA/ "https://www.flickr.com/photos/146043801@N08/") | [ ](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4c2/45c30650/92285293/6699e550/4037968354/VEsB/ "https://medium.com/@teamwarren")
---|---|---|---|---|---

name: Jill and Joe Biden
subject: Today is Memorial Day. May we spend it honoring lives and memories.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
| [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b736e/2590700157/VEsH/ "https://www.joebiden.com")
---
Over the years we have met so many incredible men and women who serve our nation, and the families who stand beside them. We've been inspired time and again by the fortitude, courage, and grit that defines this community.

We've seen them lift up their community, even after they themselves have suffered unimaginable losses. And we've seen how legacies of the fallen live on--how the memories they leave behind change the lives they touch, long after that flag has been folded and Taps has been played.

To our Gold Star families, we know your loss is an emptiness that is always felt. Yet, we hope there is comfort in the inheritance your loved one left behind: your memories, your resilience, your lives, which are shaped forever by the love you will always hold for them.

You all are the heart, soul, and spine of this nation. The Bidens stand by you today, tomorrow, and always.

May God protect our troops,

Jill, Joe, and the Biden family
|
---
[](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b736d/2590700157/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/joebiden") [](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b736c/2590700157/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/joebiden") [](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b736b/2590700157/VEsC/ "https://www.youtube.com/joebiden") [](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b736a/2590700157/VEsD/ "https://www.instagram.com/joebiden")

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you would like to receive fewer emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b7369/2590700157/VEsA/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-email"). If you would like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319beb/1cd0ac28/20d507c8/356b7368/2590700157/VEsB/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/content/Unsubscribe"). |
| | Paid for by Biden for President
---
clean_body: | "" Over the years we have met so many incredible men and women who serve our nation, and the families who stand beside them. We've been inspired time and again by the fortitude, courage, and grit that defines this community. We've seen them lift up their community, even after they themselves have suffered unimaginable losses. And we've seen how legacies of the fallen live on--how the memories they leave behind change the lives they touch, long after that flag has been folded and Taps has been played. To our Gold Star families, we know your loss is an emptiness that is always felt. Yet, we hope there is comfort in the inheritance your loved one left behind: your memories, your resilience, your lives, which are shaped forever by the love you will always hold for them. You all are the heart, soul, and spine of this nation. The Bidens stand by you today, tomorrow, and always. May God protect our troops, Jill, Joe, and the Biden family | "" "" "" ""

name: Francis Conole
subject: a quick note about Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Conole for Congress
body:
| [](conoleforcongress.com)
---

| | Friend,

This weekend is a time to reflect on the brave men and women who paid the ultimate sacrifice defending our nation. Having served the past twenty years in uniform, I am reminded of the many dear friends, classmates, and fellow servicemen and women who gave their lives in the line of duty.

This Memorial Day, as you gather with friends and family, take a moment to honor the fallen veterans and their families.

These folks are heroes who deserve all of our respect and honor.

Yours in service,

Fran |
---|---|---

|
---

_This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Francis Conole is running to represent New York 's 24th Congressional District to bring fresh leadership to Central NY. A fourth-generation Syracusan and Iraq War veteran, he has dedicated his life to service and sacrifice. name: Andrew Yang
subject: On This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=1)[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=2) Happy Memorial Day! I hope you are enjoying a wonderful day with loved ones and family. Memorial Day became a federal holiday in 1971, though it has been celebrated in some form since the 1860s. Today, many Americans—especially those who have not experienced personal loss in this arena—have a natural inclination not to look too closely when it comes to the results of the wars we are fighting or have fought. We are quick with a, "Thank you for your service." We are slow to think deeply about what it means that [less than one percent of our population serves](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=omAO4PhNPOfhCtXsiaKeG9S9O74ibB7P-_CGI0VNpYsXSMFPKTCoVVv9CTMvv-rSiQFinJkxTsCmdS2mQUBOdw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=3 "US Military Demographics"), and that even within our 50 states, [there is a marked difference in enlistment rates](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=GUadKUZbNtXpoQtrN7XiLlDAp0T1nvsEvUIUmB084zET-S2kIYZIBXbjoxx2SZQHQqfYORkMeYrZhRQNzRvmSQY3Z9mo89cWnLHvG1N7liAnb9dPmh2LsIQdvaZeVW7Y&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=4 "Enlistment Rates by State"), and that [25% of our active duty members and veterans](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=HR0zXZcA2EhSsHwYdNaPOElY_UFTH18Q_a-4zuONy9hwUIJv-xNaxbdZW1dNrIfbdBYe64xW8npcfexw0SuPYQ&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=5 "NAMI: Veterans & Active Duty") show signs of PTSD and other mental health issues. To this end, I asked a member of my staff who is a veteran what he would ask of Americans on this Memorial Day. He recommended an essay that struck a chord with me—[Phil Klay's _The Warrior at the Mall_](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=PiInCVHjZg_Lhb0RQ-dhd-f9dUtA9BFLkPKeNFGdc9IsXFjp9I5xqC5iRDo_w7IeFFUykXMjsG8fSxb2-A_ZQ2DGhEGil4WNRTiB8Hknn0c&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=6 "The Warrior at the Mall"). The essence of Mr. Klay's argument is that, as a result of the same minority of Americans spending decades at war while an overwhelming majority carries on with their lives as if nothing is amiss, a chasm has opened up between civilians and veterans. This chasm must be bridged, or, as Mr. Klay says: _"We risk our country slipping further into the practice of a fraudulent form of American patriotism, where 'soldiers are sacred,' the work of actual soldiering is ignored and the pageantry of military worship sucks energy away from the obligations of citizenship."_ Mr. Klay also points out _"Support for our military remains high at a time when respect for almost every other institution is perilously low, so pushing a military angle as a wedge makes a certain kind of sense. But ... our military is justified only by the civic life and values it exists to defend."_ That is, we don't believe in much these days beyond our military. But the military exists to defend our society itself. Thus, we owe it to our veterans to take our citizenship seriously enough that it warrants their sacrifices. If there is a way that we can celebrate this Memorial Day beyond reflecting on and thanking our veterans and members of the armed services for all they have done and given up, it would be to try to become more worthy of their sacrifice. This includes examining our decisions to put our soldiers into harm's way and how we treat and support them after they return. It even includes how we treat ourselves and other members of our society. I met with a veterans' organization this week to learn about their experiences. Both of the officials I met with served in the Middle East in the Marine Corps. Now they work at an organization that tries to end the 'Forever Wars' and advocates for Congress regaining its say on military deployments which have fallen solely to the executive branch since 2001. That is where their patriotism and service has led them. We can do better. We will do better. For our veterans and the country they sacrificed so much for. Respectfully, -Andrew
---
---
Thank you for helping to make Universal Basic Income a reality.

[Yang2020.com](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGSZwl_jDGybFpG2O3zBACL4&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=7) | [Twitter](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8TqE6xdNTSVfW5RhtXxhMjJ0&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=8) | [Facebook](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6iem_nK8ycftXP-O5PmREpZFFJK2fNzInyXSvMk1KZmg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=9) | [Instagram](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=6hyvAYXBs1PELL28GD73y48vYg4fs9ZaLi2ukqvLwvJQL5wg0UiTTe5otEcf4_Y3&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=10)

Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang.

All rights reserved.
New York, NY
#humanityfirst

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://my.yang2020.com/unsubscribe?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05272019_mem_day_2&n=11).
---

name: Dan Baer
subject: Did you know this history of Memorial Day?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
Wishing you and yours peace and progress on this Memorial Day, and let us not forget "the cost of a free and undivided republic."
|
---

| | Dear supporter,

For many of us, Memorial Day is a punctuation mark in the year that signals the start of summer, a time to gather with family for a first-of-the-season picnic or cookout.

But for all of us it is a time to be grateful for the service of those who have risked and made the ultimate sacrifice on behalf of our country. (My doctoral dissertation was actually titled "The Ultimate Sacrifice" and in it I wrote about the ethics of military service, and of the heroic decision people make to give up their lives life in defense of others.)

The roots of this holiday are traced to what was then called "Decoration Day" and first commemorated in 1868 in the wake of the Civil War on the orders of General Logan who was then the Commander-in-Chief of the Grand Army of the Republic. (He went on to be a U.S. Senator from Illinois, and If you've ever visited Washington DC, you may have seen his statue in the middle of Logan Circle there.)

In Logan's original General Order 11, he said that "no form of ceremony is prescribed" for the remembrance of those who had sacrificed. Today, there are many commemorations of Memorial Day, but each of us can choose a way to acknowledge the sacrifices of those who have lost their lives.

In his order Logan also wrote:

_"Let no vandalism of avarice or neglect, no ravages of time testify to the present or to the coming generations that we have forgotten as a people the cost of a free and undivided republic."_

We are living through a difficult era for our country: the "vandalism of avarice and neglect" emanates daily from the White House. Divisions seem to be advancing rather than receding, our journey toward a more perfect union seems to be receding rather than advancing. We are at an inflection point. That's why I'm running, and why I'm grateful for your support.

The best way we can honor the sacrifices of the past is by remaining defiantly optimistic about our future and committed to building a country that delivers life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness for all.

Wishing you and yours peace and progress on this Memorial Day, and let us not forget "the cost of a free and undivided republic."

Onward,

Dan (and Brian) | | **[**DONATE**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cb/232dfd1/92fc1c8/62a16115/2242564916/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190527_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
---

** **
|
---
[](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cb/232dfd1/92fc1c8/62a1611a/2242564916/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/") [](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cb/232dfd1/92fc1c8/62a1611b/2242564916/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/danbbaer")

Dan for Colorado
PO Box 1199
Denver, CO 80201

[Unsubscribe](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cb/232dfd1/92fc1c8/62a16118/2242564916/VEsC/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/unsubscribe")

| | Paid for by Dan for Colorado

name: Gary Peters
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN
body:
|
---
| This Memorial Day, I hope you'll join me in remembering the men and women who bravely served in uniform and made the ultimate sacrifice for this country. Our gratitude is as enduring as the nation and ideals they protected.

Millions of servicemembers have put themselves in harm's way to protect our way of life and the safety of our nation. For that, we owe each of them and their families a debt of gratitude that can never truly be repaid.

Let us reflect on their selfless service and honor - today and every day.

Sincerely,

Gary
---

| Peters for Michigan
Po Box 32072
Detroit, MI 48244
---
PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN
Senator Gary Peters stands up for middle-class families in Michigan and across America every day. He is fighting for quality education, increasing job growth, and protecting our values. Gary is working hard to stand up to the extreme Trump agenda and turn the Senate blue and the path to taking back Congress runs right through Michigan.

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: On Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Each year on Memorial Day, we remember the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country, our families, and our ideals.
| | Peter,

Each year on Memorial Day, we remember the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country, our families, and our ideals. American service members have laid down their lives so that we can enjoy the liberties and freedoms we hold so dear as Americans.

Words can never convey our gratitude for their sacrifice, and no amount of thanks can begin to repay the debt we owe.

Peter, on this Memorial Day, join me in honoring those who have paid the ultimate price in defense of our nation and please keep those they left behind -- our Gold Star families -- in our hearts and prayers. Together, let's endeavor to fully appreciate the freedoms they fought to preserve and defend the Constitution they gave their lives to protect.

Remembering today and every day,

_Abigail_
---
| Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/xzEk7XInQD0VItYxm31eXLPiF6b_jRaYeV306stPNh4=/HVO0amvqNlKdft_4UZD3jwHngRHsAdt17T7muWB_3Eo=)

clean_body: Each year on Memorial Day, we remember the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country, our families, and our ideals. | | Peter, Each year on Memorial Day, we remember the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country, our families, and our ideals. American service members have laid down their lives so that we can enjoy the liberties and freedoms we hold so dear as Americans. Words can never convey our gratitude for their sacrifice, and no amount of thanks can begin to repay the debt we owe. Peter, on this Memorial Day, join me in honoring those who have paid the ultimate price in defense of our nation and please keep those they left behind -- our Gold Star families -- in our hearts and prayers. Together, let's endeavor to fully appreciate the freedoms they fought to preserve and defend the Constitution they gave their lives to protect. Remembering today and every day, Abigail | Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together.
